title: IoT Device Fleet Health Dashboard
app: IoT platform API, MQTT broker metrics
spl:
index=iot sourcetype="iot_platform:devices"
| eval health=case(status!="online","offline", battery_pct<15,"low_battery", rssi< -110,"poor_rssi", 1=1,"ok")
| stats count by health, product_family
| sort health
description: Single pane for battery, RSSI, last-seen, and firmware supports NOC and field operations.
value: Single pane for battery, RSSI, last-seen, and firmware supports NOC and field operations.
implementation: Normalize vendor fields. Refresh dashboard every 5 min. Drill to device detail.
visualization: Status grid (region × health), Treemap (fleet by family), Single value (% healthy).
knownFalsePositives: Battery alarms during scheduled load tests, planned battery replacements, or after a sustained brownout recovery.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: IoT platform API, MQTT broker metrics.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="iot_platform:devices"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Normalize vendor fields. Refresh dashboard every 5 min. Drill to device detail.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iot sourcetype="iot_platform:devices"
| eval health=case(status!="online","offline", battery_pct<15,"low_battery", rssi< -110,"poor_rssi", 1=1,"ok")
| stats count by health, product_family
| sort health
```

Understanding this SPL

**IoT Device Fleet Health Dashboard** — Single pane for battery, RSSI, last-seen, and firmware supports NOC and field operations.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="iot_platform:devices"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): IoT platform API, MQTT broker metrics. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: iot; **sourcetype**: iot_platform:devices. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=iot, sourcetype="iot_platform:devices". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **health** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by health, product_family** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare device counts, last-seen times, and health fields to the IoT platform console (for example AWS IoT, Azure IoT Hub, or Google Cloud IoT) for the same period. Verify API polling intervals and that offline rules match your operational definition.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (region × health), Treemap (fleet by family), Single value (% healthy).
grandmaExplanation: We watch your connected devices and the messages they send. We help you know if something is off with batteries, signal, and which sensors have gone quiet before harm, damage, or wasted effort piles up.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable (no CIM or Operational_Telemetry data model in this SPL).
